meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Freelance Pitching to Solve Project Burnout
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your coding projects. Learn how to use freelance pitching to gain accountability and build a professional portfolio with Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of external stakes. By pitching your app as a freelance solution to a real client, you force completion through contractual obligation. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to execute the build, turning 'learning projects' into 'shippable products'.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: a folder full of half-baked repos, abandoned READMEs, and the lingering sting of 'almost finished.' The screen flickers with a half-functioning UI, and the motivation to push that final commit has evaporated. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack a deadline that bites.

Freelance pitching is the ultimate hack for the student developer. When you stop building for yourself and start building for a client, the project shifts from a hobby to a professional necessity. By leveraging AI-assisted workflows, you can compress months of development into weeks, transforming your portfolio from a graveyard of ideas into a showcase of successful deployments.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Viable Pitch
steps.description: Identify a small business problem you can solve in 14 days. Focus on high-value, low-complexity features like automated email reports or data scrapers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a technical project manager. Analyze the following business problem: [Insert Problem]. Break it down into a 14-day development roadmap suitable for a student freelancer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --plan 'Draft a 14-day project roadmap for [Project Name]'

steps.title: Architect the MVP with Antigravity
steps.description: Don't over-engineer. Use Antigravity to generate a clean, modular file structure that allows for rapid feature iteration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular Next.js project structure for a freelance tool. Prioritize simplicity, maintainability, and clear separation of concerns for a beginner developer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project scaffolding based on the Antigravity architecture plan'

steps.title: Execute Core Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write your backend logic and database connections. Focus on getting the 'happy path' working first.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust API route for [Feature] that handles edge cases and includes basic error logging for a production-ready freelance app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the API endpoint for [Feature] and add unit tests'

steps.title: Automate the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Integrate a simple feedback mechanism so your client can review progress without you having to manually deploy every change.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script to generate a temporary preview URL for my local environment so a non-technical client can review the current build.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Setup ngrok or Vercel preview deployment script for client review'

steps.title: Final Polish and Hand-off
steps.description: Clean up your repository and documentation. A professional hand-off is what leads to referrals and future work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README.md for this project, highlighting the technical stack, setup instructions, and the specific business problem solved.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate documentation and clean up unused dependencies'

pro_tips: Always charge a nominal fee even for your first project; it changes the client's psychological investment in the outcome.
Use Claude Code to generate 'self-documenting' code comments; it makes your code look senior-level to any future employer reviewing your GitHub.
Never build features outside the original scope until the MVP is fully deployed; scope creep is the number one project killer.
Record a 2-minute Loom video of your finished project; it's more effective for your portfolio than a thousand lines of code.
Treat your local development environment like production; use environment variables and structured logging from day one.
content.next_steps: Once you deploy your first project, create a 'Case Study' page on your portfolio site detailing the problem, your solution, and the tech stack. Then, find your next client on LinkedIn or local community boards.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop building for the void and start building for the market. Stop procrastinating on your own projects and start solving real problems for real people. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
